channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: As quakes rattle Greek islands, a few brave tourists enjoy having Santorini to themselves
item.description: SANTORINI, Greece (AP) — More Greek islands closed schools Tuesday as hundreds of earthquakes ratted the Aegean Sea, while a handful of hardy tourists enjoyed having Santorini’s stunning views to themselves.
item.dc:creator: DEREK GATOPOULOS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump and Netanyahu hold talks as US president warns 'no guarantees' fragile peace in Gaza will hold
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu are set to meet Tuesday as the Israeli prime minister faces competing pressure from his right-wing coalition to end a temporary truce against Hamas militants in Gaza and from war-weary Israelis who want the remaining hostages home and t
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Modi's ruling party looks to topple corruption crusader in Delhi poll with graft allegations
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — The resurgent Hindu nationalist party of India's Prime Minister Narendra Modi is eyeing a state legislature election in Delhi where it is looking to unseat the governing movement which has led the Indian capital for over a decade but has recently been embroiled in graft allegations.
item.dc:creator: ASHOK SHARMA and SHEIKH SAALIQ, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trayon White channels Marion Barry as he fights corruption charges and expulsion from the DC Council
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Trayon White came up in local politics as a direct protege of iconic and infamous former Mayor Marion Barry. Now the D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration opens antisemitism inquiries at 5 colleges including Columbia and Berkeley
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Trump administration is opening new investigations into allegations of antisemitism at five U.S. universities including Columbia and the University of California, Berkeley, the Education Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary deportation protection for 350,000 Venezuelans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump's administration is ending protections that shielded roughly 350,000 Venezuelans from deportation, leaving them with two months before they lose their right to work in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA and GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Duffy tells DOT to prioritize areas with high birth rates
item.description: Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy in a memo told the Department of Transportation (DOT) to prioritize communities that have higher birth and marriage rates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Gerber recalls all batches of Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks nationwide
item.description: Gerber Products Company has announced a nationwide recall and discontinuation of all batches of its Soothe N Chew Teething Sticks due to a potential choking hazard for infants and young children.
item.dc:creator: Reagan Wynn, KRON4

item.title: Tariff threats take aim at fentanyl trafficking. Here's how the drug reaches the US
item.description: President Donald Trump's plan to impose tariffs on goods from Mexico, Canada and China is partly aimed at combating the illicit flow of fentanyl into the U.S., where the opioid is blamed for some 70,000 overdose deaths annually. Mexico agreed Monday to send 10,000 troops to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: JESSE BEDAYN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Transportation secretary says 'expansive' impact zone was left by Philadelphia medical plane crash
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — The impact area where a medical transportation plane crashed in Philadelphia was notably “expansive,” U.S. Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy said Monday after viewing the site with investigators and city officials. Duffy said he was struck by “how expansive it was.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Judge orders acquittal of ex-prosecutor on 1 of 2 counts in misconduct trial over Ahmaud Arbery case
item.description: BRUNSWICK, Ga. (AP) — A judge on Monday ordered the acquittal of a former Georgia prosecutor on one of the two misconduct charges against her, ruling that prosecutors at her trial failed to present any evidence the she obstructed a police investigation of the 2020 killing of Ahmaud .Arbery.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ayesha Curry’s Oakland shop to close citing safety concerns
item.description: The boutique shop was opened in 2021 and features products from Black-owned businesses as well as local Bay Area makers.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mense, NewsNation

item.title: What is known about the deadly collision between a passenger jet and Army helicopter
item.description: American Airlines Flight 5342 and an Army helicopter collided in midair near Washington D.C.'s Reagan National Wednesday night, sending the two aircraft into the Potomac River and killing all 67 aboard in the deadliest U.S. air disaster since 2001.
item.dc:creator: JOHN RABY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Thune on Trump tariffs: 'We’ll see how long' they last
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) told reporters Monday he’s taking a wait-and-see approach to President Trump’s order to place 25-percent tariffs on Canada and Mexico and a 10-percent tariff on China, which sent stock markets dropping.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Recall for broccoli sold at Walmart in 20 states upgraded to highest risk level: FDA
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration has upgraded its classification of a broccoli recall initially reported in late December, labeling it as “Class I” — the agency’s most serious recall category.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Officer shoots suspect carrying rifle toward Memphis elementary school
item.description: MEMPHIS, Tenn. -- A man is in critical condition after an officer-involved shooting near an elementary school in the Vollintine-Evergreen area Monday morning. At around 10:45 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Megan Fayard, NewsNation

item.title: Trump doubles down on floating Canada as 51st state amid tariff dispute
item.description: President Trump on Monday reiterated his desire to see Canada become part of the United States as he prepared to impose steep tariffs on Canadian goods.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Number injured following Philadelphia plane crash increases to 24, some remain hospitalized
item.description: Philadelphia's mayor and U.S. Department of Transportation Secretary Sean Duffy spoke at a news conference Monday alongside investigators of the deadly medical jet crash investigation.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Marco Rubio taking over as acting head of USAID amid agency turmoil
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio is being put in charge of the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID) as the Trump administration mulls the future of the agency.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Dozens of Education Department staffers on leave amid Trump's DEI purge
item.description: Dozens of employees with the Department of Education were placed on administrative leave on Friday, allegedly after attending a diversity training course encouraged by then-Secretary Betsy DeVos during the first Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: American bald eagles are having a moment, ecologically and culturally
item.description: WEST ORANGE, N.J. (AP) — Along the long road from American icon to endangered species and back again, the bald eagle — the national bird of the United States, often seen against a clear blue sky — is having a moment.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Minnesota man accused of holding girlfriend against her will, beating her, dumping boiling water on her
item.description: A Minnesota man allegedly held his 19-year-old girlfriend against her will, beat her and repeatedly dumped boiling hot water on her over several days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mayor says 5 injured in fatal Philadelphia plane crash remain hospitalized, 3 in critical condition
item.description: PHILADELPHIA (AP) — Three people injured on the ground when a jet crashed in a busy Philadelphia neighborhood, killing seven people, remain in critical condition, Mayor Cherelle Parker said Sunday. Parker said 22 people were injured and five of them remain hospitalized.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Airport employee injured in collision with aircraft wing at Chicago's O'Hare
item.description: The driver of a tug vehicle at Chicago O'Hare International Airport was injured Saturday evening after colliding with the wing of an airplane.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: Grammy Awards: How to watch the ceremony, red carpet arrivals, interviews
item.description: The 67th annual Grammy Awards are set to recognize some of the most talented performers and biggest hits in pop music on Sunday, with different ways to watch the festivities.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Will La Niña be a 'bust' this winter? Meteorologist explains why it may feel like it
item.description: After months of teasing, a La Niña winter has finally started – but we don't blame you if you haven't noticed.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, ABC 4

item.title: Passengers, crew killed in 'high-impact' air ambulance crash in Philadelphia identified
item.description: Authorities said a seventh person, who had been in a car at the time of the crash, has died. Their identity has not yet been released.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WGN 9

item.title: Canadian fans boo U.S. National Anthem at hockey game amid trade war
item.description: Canadians aren’t too happy with the tariffs being imposed on their country by U.S. President Donald Trump. Trump signed an order Saturday to impose stiff tariffs on imports from Canada as well as Mexico and China, which drew swift retaliation and sparked a trade war.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: China to file lawsuit with WTO, vows 'countermeasures' in response to Trump tariffs
item.description: In a statement, the ministry’s spokesperson vowed further “countermeasures” against the United States but did not specify what steps it would take.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: A 2,500th 3-pointer, a triple-double, Bronny's basket give LeBron James another memorable MSG game
item.description: A 2,500th 3-pointer for his career and his 10th triple-double this season. Not a bad 31st trip to Madison Square Garden for LeBron James. Pretty good first one for Bronny James, too.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MAHONEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Michigan priest has license revoked after imitating Musk's gesture at Pro-Life Summit
item.description: The priest, Calvin Robinson, attended the National Pro-Life Summit in Washington, D.C. last month. Toward the end of his remarks at the Jan. 25 event, he mimicked Musk’s salute, which made the rounds online.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Data from the deadliest U.S. air accident in a generation show conflicting altitude readings
item.description: Recovery crews and divers searched the Potomac River for remains and cleared wreckage Saturday from the midair collision of a passenger jet and Army helicopter that killed 67 people.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mid-air collision draws attention to military helicopter training, previous accidents
item.description: To learn to fly a military helicopter is to take a master class in multitasking. You need both hands and both feet to control the aircraft while keeping a close eye on the horizon and your altitude.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, ABC 4
